parts.instruction: Questions 11–12 and 13–14: Choose TWO A–E. Questions 15–20: Matching A–H.

parts.instruction: Questions 21–22 and 23–24: Choose TWO A–E. Questions 25–30: Choose A, B or C.

questions.prompt: Which TWO points does Adam make about his experiment on artificial sweeteners?

options.label: The results were what he had predicted.
The experiment was simple to set up.
A large sample of people was tested.
The subjects were unaware of what they were drinking.
The test was repeated several times for each person.

questions.prompt: Which TWO points does Adam make about his experiment on artificial sweeteners?

options.label: The results were what he had predicted.
The experiment was simple to set up.

questions.prompt: Which TWO problems did Rosie have when measuring the fat content of nuts?

options.label: She used the wrong sort of nuts.
She did not grind the nuts finely enough.
The information on the nut package was incorrect.
The weighing scales may have been unsuitable.

questions.prompt: Adam suggests that restaurants could reduce obesity if their menus

options.label: were organised in a particular way.

questions.sectionInstruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.prompt: The students agree that food manufacturers deliberately

options.label: make calorie counts hard to understand.
fail to provide accurate calorie counts.
use ineffective methods to reduce calories.

questions.prompt: What does Rosie say about levels of exercise in England?

options.label: The amount recommended is much too low.
Most people overestimate how much they do.
Women now exercise more than they used to.

questions.prompt: Adam refers to the location and width of stairs in a train station to illustrate

options.label: practical changes that can influence people's behaviour.
methods of helping people who have mobility problems.
ways of preventing accidents by controlling crowd movement.

questions.prompt: What do the students agree about including reference to exercise in their presentation?

options.label: They should probably leave it out.
They need to do more research on it.
They should discuss this with their tutor.

questions.prompt: What are the students going to do next for their presentation?

options.label: find out how long they have for it
decide on its content and organisation
